question: What are you wanting right now?
What has been quietly pulling your attention lately?
What’s something small you’ve been craving this week?
When do you feel most alive in your day-to-day life?
What kind of experiences tend to wake you up?
What’s something you’re looking forward to right now?
What do you find yourself thinking about when your mind wanders?
What kind of environments make you feel more open or receptive?
What does desire feel like in your body?
Where in your life do you feel a sense of movement or pull?
What’s something you want but haven’t acted on yet?
What kind of attention makes you feel more awake?
When was the last time you felt strongly drawn toward something?
What does it feel like to sit with a desire without acting on it?
Where do you feel the most energy or aliveness in your life right now?
What’s something that surprised you by how much you wanted it?
Do you think desire is something you choose, or something that happens to you?
Is wanting something a form of lack, or a form of connection?
What do you think desire is trying to do in a human life?
Do you trust your desires?
What happens when you ignore a desire?
Is fulfillment the end of desire, or something else?
Do you think desire changes as we get older?
What’s the relationship between desire and meaning for you?
“Love is the desire for the perpetual possession of the good.” — Plato. What do you think he means by that?
“Eros is a great spirit, a mediator between the human and the divine.” — Plato, Symposium. Does desire feel like a bridge for you?
“Where there is desire, there is going to be a flame.” — Rumi. What kind of flame do you notice in your life?
“Desire is half of life; indifference is half of death.” — Kahlil Gibran. Where do you feel alive versus indifferent?
“Tell me what you pay attention to and I will tell you who you are.” — José Ortega y Gasset. How does this relate to desire for you?
What’s something you wanted deeply as a child?
What did wanting feel like to you when you were younger?
What did you long for before you knew what was realistic?
What kinds of desires came naturally to you as a kid?
When did you first become aware of holding yourself back from wanting something?
What did you want that you never expressed growing up?
How has your relationship to wanting changed over time?
Do you feel more or less connected to desire than you did as a child?
What’s something you’ve wanted for a long time?
What’s a desire you’ve outgrown?
What’s a desire that has stayed with you through different phases of life?
What’s something you wanted that ended up changing you?
What’s a desire you followed that surprised you?
What’s something you didn’t get that ended up mattering?
What’s a moment where you felt completely pulled toward something?
What’s something you’re still waiting for?
Have you ever wanted something you knew would change your life?
What’s a desire you’ve ignored that keeps returning?
When has wanting something taught you more than having it?
Do you tend to move toward your desires or away from them?
Where do you hold yourself back from wanting more?
What kind of desires feel easiest for you to admit?
Which desires feel hardest to acknowledge?
How do you feel when you see someone else fully alive in their desire?
Do you enjoy witnessing other people’s passions and longings?
Is there someone in your life whose desire you admire?
How do you respond when someone you love wants something deeply?
Have you ever felt threatened by someone else’s desire?
What does it feel like to support someone else’s longing?
Do you think desire connects people or separates them?
What’s something you love seeing other people care about?
If desire had a shape or image for you right now, what would it be?
If you could follow one desire without consequence, what would it be?
What would your life look like if you trusted your desires more?
What feels just out of reach in a way that excites you?
